verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass TM glycoprotein stably anchored to the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane via its extracellular N-terminal domain. It is a bona-fide surface protein. However, expression is restricted to the male germline (spermatids/spermatozoa) and is essentially absent from all somatic tissues. Per triage rules, surface display restricted to narrow germline/developmental lineages qualifies as contextual/tissue_restricted_surface, not yes, even when the anchor (TM, GPI) is unambiguous in that compartment.
